title: Acceptance vs. Rejection at the Same Level
concept: The same break of the same level resolves into opposite trades depending on whether the auction accepts the new prices (time, volume, value migration) or rejects them (fast probe, snap-back, excess tail).
teaching_objective: Judge a break as acceptance or rejection from time, volume, and behavior beyond the level, not from a price cross or a single candle close.
junior_error: Verdict-by-touch: declaring the break real because price crossed the line or one candle closed above it, which means buying every rejection at its extreme.
professional_read: Acceptance shows successive periods building volume beyond the reference, value migrating through it, pullbacks holding above where passive demand rebuilds, and two-sided business. Rejection shows a thin fast probe, the book above never lifted, a snap-back inside the range within minutes, negative delta answering the breakout buyers, and a single-print excess tail.
decision_consequence: Acceptance authorizes trading with the break (continuation long above a held former resistance). Rejection flips the bias to a responsive short against the trapped breakout longs, who become the move's fuel.

required_evidence.label: Time and volume built beyond the level
Value area / POC migration relative to the level
Pullback holds above vs snap-back through
Profile shape: fattened vs single-print excess tail

forbidden_errors.description: Do not depict a single price cross or one candle close above the level as acceptance.
Do not show acceptance without successive periods of building time and volume.
Do not imply rejection and acceptance resolve on the same timescale; rejection is fast, acceptance is slow to prove.
Do not omit that rejection traps the breakout longs and converts them into fuel.

panels.title: Profile at the level (toggles by state)

sequence.caption: Acceptance: 40 minutes building 21,560-21,585, value migrating up through 21,560, pullbacks holding 21,561-63.
Rejection: 90-second spike to 21,576 on thinning prints, back to 21,552 in 4 minutes, single prints 21,562-21,576.

annotations.text: Same level, same break time (10:05). The level does not decide; the response does.
Rejection state: trapped breakout longs above 21,560 are the short's fuel.

fixture.description: Two NQ sequences at prior-day high 21,560, both breaking at 10:05. ACCEPT: lift to 21,574, then 40 minutes building 21,560-21,585 with volume fattening at 21,568, pullbacks bottoming 21,561-63 with bid refresh, developing VAH migrating up through 21,560. REJECT: 90-second spike to 21,576 on thinning prints, book above 21,576 never lifted, return to 21,552 within 4 minutes with deeply negative delta, single prints 21,562-21,576 leaving an excess tail.
fixture.notes: Counts and prices are fixed and bounded; static teaching fixture, renders in SVG without Canvas. The toggle exposes exactly two states (accept, reject) over an identical level reference.

acceptance_criteria: Both states render against the same level reference (21,560), so the comparison is genuinely same-level.
All five evidence items are drawn for each state with opposing values.
The acceptance state shows value migrating through the level; the rejection state shows value anchored inside plus a single-print excess tail.
The figure never depicts a single price cross or one candle close as acceptance.
The rejection state shows the trapped breakout longs as the consequence.
The toggle switches between exactly two states, not more.
